title: cornus controversa var. variegata
intro: Cornus controversa var. variegata, also known as the wedding cake tree, is a variegated cultivar of the cornus controversa plant species, which is a type of flowering dogwood tree native to East Asia. The bark of the Cornus controversa var. variegata plant contains medicinal properties, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Cornus controversa var. variegata include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules for medicinal purposes.
